growth_records.medium: riboflavin fermentation medium (composition in Supplementary Method 1; not in full text)

sec.method: HPLC (peak area at 370 nm); 5-L fed-batch bioreactor
derived: 5.03 g/L / 376.36 g/mol = 13.36 mmol/L; /28 h = 0.477 mmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: Zhiheng Yang et al. A thermostable type I-B CRISPR-Cas system for orthogonal and multiplexed genetic engineering. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-41973-5. [PMC10551041]
growth_records.snippet: Finally, in a 5-L bioreactor, the performance of the engineered Gt-09 strain produced riboflavin with a final titer of 5.03 g/L within 28 h ... which represents the highest riboflavin titer achieved in thermophilic P. thermoglucosidasius.
growth_records.notes: Genetic-tool / metabolic-engineering paper, NOT a GEM/MFA physiology paper. Only PRODUCT TITERS are reported for engineered riboflavin strains; no substrate uptake rate, no O2 rate, and no numerical specific growth rate (mu) are given, and no per-biomass (gDW) normalisation is possible because biomass at harvest is not reported. Growth was only 'monitored by OD600' (EnSpire plate reader) with no mu value stated. Riboflavin MW = 376.36 g/mol used for molar conversion. Derived volumetric rates: 5.03 g/L / 28 h = 0.1796 g/L/h; 5.03/376.36 = 13.36 mmol/L, /28 h = 0.477 mmol/L/h (these are VOLUMETRIC, not specific mmol/gDW/h, and are marked is_experimental:false because they are computed, not measured). Shake-flask endpoint titers for earlier strains (no fermentation time given, so no rate derivable): Gt-06 = 421 mg/L, Gt-08 = 521 mg/L, Gt-09 = 719 mg/L (all in 250 mL shake flasks, 250 rpm, 60 C). Fermentation medium composition is only in Supplementary Method 1 (not in the retrieved full text), so medium_composition_stated:false. Bioreactor temperature/pH/aeration not stated in full text (shake-flask fermentations were 60 C). Record retained for completeness of an engineered-prokaryote secretion observation; low confidence and NOT suitable as a quantitative GEM exchange-flux constraint. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 1430 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/1430
